summary:
Heparin activates antithrombin III, inhibiting thrombin and factor Xa; it also directly interferes by preventing the conversion of fibrinogen into fibrin, thus stopping blood clot formation. To clarify from prior statements, both mechanisms are crucial for understanding how heparin works clinically. ```plaintext
Heparin mainly functions by activating antithrombin III to block thrombin and factor Xa, thereby hindering the transformation of fibrinogen into fibrin and halting clot development.
```
formatted_summary:
agent2 (Round 2 summarized): Heparin activates antithrombin III, inhibiting thrombin and factor Xa; it also directly interferes by preventing the conversion of fibrinogen into fibrin, thus stopping blood clot formation. To clarify from prior statements, both mechanisms are crucial for understanding how heparin works clinically. ```plaintext
Heparin mainly functions by activating antithrombin III to block thrombin and factor Xa, thereby hindering the transformation of fibrinogen into fibrin and halting clot development.
```. Answer choice: A
